_meta.title: AI & Data Vendors NOT at Snowflake Summit 2026
_meta.method: 19-segment parallel research swarm (Claude Code workflow) + completeness critic; deduped and cross-checked against the 197 Snowflake Summit 2026 partner vendors.
_meta.caveat: First-pass competitive-landscape map. Funding/valuation/HQ are best-effort from public sources and need validation before external use.
_meta.audited: 2026-06-07: 14-agent audit — applied relationship/tier corrections; pruned hallucinated-scratch rows + absorbed/defunct vendors

vendors.name: Databricks
vendors.one_liner: Lakehouse platform unifying data engineering, warehousing, ML and AI on open formats.
vendors.notable_investors: Thrive Capital, a16z, Andreessen Horowitz, GIC, Insight Partners, T. Rowe Price
vendors.why_notable: The #1 head-to-head competitor to Snowflake; defines the lakehouse category.

vendors.name: Google BigQuery
vendors.one_liner: Serverless, fully managed cloud data warehouse with separated storage and compute.
vendors.why_notable: One of the three hyperscaler warehouses and a top-3 Snowflake alternative.

vendors.name: Amazon Redshift
vendors.one_liner: AWS-native MPP cloud data warehouse with serverless option and S3 lakehouse querying.
vendors.why_notable: The default warehouse for AWS-committed enterprises; major Snowflake competitor.

vendors.name: Microsoft Fabric / Azure Synapse
vendors.one_liner: Microsoft's unified analytics and warehousing SaaS spanning OneLake, warehouse and BI.
vendors.why_notable: Fabric (successor to Synapse) is Microsoft's bundled Snowflake/Databricks challenger.

vendors.name: ClickHouse
vendors.one_liner: Open-source columnar OLAP database and cloud for sub-second real-time analytics.
vendors.total_funding: ~$650M+ raised; $400M Series D (Jan 2026) led by Dragoneer, ~$6B+ valuation
vendors.hq: San Francisco, CA, USA (incorporated US; founder Russian origin)
vendors.notable_investors: Dragoneer, Bessemer, Index Ventures, Khosla, Lightspeed, GIC, T. Rowe Price
vendors.why_notable: Fastest-growing real-time analytics DB (ARR +250% YoY, 3,000+ Cloud customers).

vendors.name: Firebolt
vendors.one_liner: Cloud data warehouse engineered for very fast, low-cost analytics at scale.
vendors.notable_investors: Zeev Ventures, TLV Partners, Bessemer, Dawn Capital, K5 Global, Angular Ventures
vendors.why_notable: Israeli unicorn positioning as a faster, cheaper Snowflake/Redshift for data apps.

vendors.name: MotherDuck
vendors.one_liner: Serverless analytics cloud built on DuckDB for fast, low-cost SQL.
vendors.notable_investors: Felicis, a16z, Madrona, Amplify Partners, Altimeter, Redpoint, Battery
vendors.why_notable: Commercializes DuckDB; benchmarks ~6-7x faster than Snowflake/Redshift at price.

vendors.name: DuckDB Labs
vendors.one_liner: Creators of DuckDB, the in-process columnar OLAP database engine.
vendors.why_notable: DuckDB is the 'SQLite for analytics' powering a fast-growing local-first OLAP wave.

vendors.name: SingleStore
vendors.one_liner: Distributed SQL database unifying transactions and real-time analytics (HTAP).
vendors.notable_investors: Vector Capital (owner), formerly Insight Partners, Khosla, GV, Dell Capital
vendors.why_notable: Leading HTAP engine for low-latency analytics; data-warehouse augmentation play.

vendors.name: Teradata
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise cloud analytics and data warehouse platform (VantageCloud).
vendors.why_notable: Incumbent enterprise warehouse; cloud ARR >$600M, classic Snowflake rival.

vendors.name: Vertica (OpenText)
vendors.one_liner: Columnar MPP analytical database for hybrid-cloud data warehousing.
vendors.why_notable: Long-standing high-performance analytical DB; deployable any-cloud and on-prem.

vendors.name: Yellowbrick Data
vendors.one_liner: Elastic SQL data warehouse running cloud-native and on-prem via Kubernetes.
vendors.notable_investors: Samsung, DFJ Growth, Menlo Ventures, GV, Threshold Ventures, Next47
vendors.why_notable: Differentiates on price/performance and full data sovereignty (any cloud + on-prem).

vendors.name: Ocient
vendors.one_liner: Hyperscale data warehouse for trillions-of-records, compute-intensive analytics.
vendors.notable_investors: Greenline Partners, OCA Ventures, In-Q-Tel, Allstate
vendors.why_notable: Targets the largest, most compute-heavy analytical workloads others can't run.

vendors.name: Exasol
vendors.one_liner: In-memory MPP analytics database known for benchmark-leading query speed.
vendors.why_notable: Leading European in-memory analytics DB; frequently tops TPC-style benchmarks.

vendors.name: StarTree
vendors.one_liner: Managed real-time analytics platform commercializing Apache Pinot.
vendors.notable_investors: GGV Capital, Bain Capital Ventures, Sapphire Ventures, CRV, Citi Ventures
vendors.why_notable: Pinot's commercial home; powers high-concurrency, user-facing real-time analytics.

vendors.name: Imply Data
vendors.one_liner: Real-time analytics database built on Apache Druid for sub-second queries.
vendors.notable_investors: Thoma Bravo, a16z, Bessemer, Khosla Ventures, OMERS
vendors.why_notable: Druid's commercial vendor; a leading real-time OLAP engine for event analytics.

vendors.name: Tinybird
vendors.one_liner: Real-time analytics platform on ClickHouse, publishing data as APIs.
vendors.notable_investors: Balderton Capital, CRV, Singular, Crane Venture Partners
vendors.why_notable: Spanish scale-up turning ClickHouse into instant analytical APIs for developers.

vendors.name: Greenplum (Broadcom/VMware)
vendors.one_liner: Open-source MPP data warehouse built on PostgreSQL for large-scale analytics.
vendors.why_notable: Veteran Postgres-based MPP warehouse still widely deployed for on-prem analytics.

vendors.name: Onehouse
vendors.one_liner: Managed universal data lakehouse built on Apache Hudi with open table formats.
vendors.why_notable: Hudi's commercial vendor; cross-engine lakehouse interop (Iceberg/Delta/Hudi).

vendors.name: CelerData (StarRocks)
vendors.one_liner: Cloud-native real-time analytics engine commercializing open-source StarRocks.
vendors.hq: Silicon Valley (Mountain View area), CA, USA
vendors.notable_investors: GGV Capital, Sequoia, Hillhouse, Lightspeed (China-origin investors)
vendors.why_notable: StarRocks is a fast-rising open OLAP/lakehouse engine; strong BI + Iceberg story.

vendors.name: VeloDB (Apache Doris)
vendors.one_liner: Commercial real-time data warehouse powered by open-source Apache Doris.
vendors.funding_stage: Private (early; SelectDB predecessor raised in China)
vendors.notable_investors: IDG Capital, Sequoia Capital China (via SelectDB/Flywheel Data)
vendors.why_notable: Apache Doris's commercial home; major China-origin real-time warehouse/OLAP player.

vendors.name: Databend (Datafuse Labs)
vendors.one_liner: Open-source, Rust-built cloud data warehouse with object-storage-native architecture.
vendors.notable_investors: Source Code Capital, Sequoia Capital China
vendors.why_notable: Rust-based open Snowflake alternative; adopted at ByteDance, Meituan, NetEase.

vendors.name: Materialize
vendors.one_liner: Streaming SQL database delivering real-time incrementally-maintained views.
vendors.why_notable: Differential-dataflow engine offering always-fresh materialized analytics.

vendors.name: RisingWave
vendors.one_liner: Cloud-native streaming database for real-time stream processing in SQL.
vendors.notable_investors: Yunqi Partners, gaming-firm investment arm, others
vendors.why_notable: Postgres-compatible streaming DB; stream-batch unification + vector/AI features.

vendors.name: e6data
vendors.one_liner: High-performance lakehouse compute engine for SQL and AI workloads.
vendors.hq: San Francisco, CA, USA (R&D in Bengaluru, India)
vendors.why_notable: Vendor-agnostic compute engine cutting lakehouse cost ~halving spend, no lock-in.

vendors.name: Kinetica
vendors.one_liner: GPU-accelerated real-time analytical database for spatial and temporal data.
vendors.notable_investors: Canvas Ventures, Citi Ventures, GreatPoint Ventures, Meritech
vendors.why_notable: GPU-vectorized DB for real-time spatiotemporal and now generative-AI analytics.

vendors.name: TileDB
vendors.one_liner: Universal multi-dimensional array database for analytics, genomics and AI.
vendors.notable_investors: Two Bear Capital, Nexus Venture Partners, Big Pi, Intel Capital
vendors.why_notable: Array-native lakehouse strong in genomics, geospatial and scientific analytics.

vendors.name: MariaDB (ColumnStore / SkySQL)
vendors.one_liner: Open-source database vendor with columnar analytics engine and managed cloud.
vendors.hq: Redwood City, CA, USA / Helsinki, Finland
vendors.why_notable: ColumnStore + SkySQL bring columnar analytical warehousing to the MariaDB base.

vendors.name: SAP HANA Cloud
vendors.one_liner: In-memory multi-model database underpinning SAP's data warehousing cloud.
vendors.why_notable: In-memory engine behind SAP Datasphere; major enterprise warehouse alternative. (Note: SAP corporate was a Summit partner; listed here for the distinct HANA Cloud DB engine.)

vendors.name: Striim
vendors.one_liner: Real-time data streaming and CDC integration platform for analytics and operations.
vendors.notable_investors: Goldman Sachs Growth Equity, Summit Partners, Atlantic Bridge, Dell Ventures, Bosch Ventures
vendors.why_notable: Leading independent enterprise log-based CDC/streaming vendor; direct GoldenGate/Qlik Replicate alternative for low-latency pipelines.

vendors.name: SnapLogic
vendors.one_liner: AI-powered low-code iPaaS for data and application integration pipelines.
vendors.notable_investors: Sixth Street Growth, Vitruvian Partners, Andreessen Horowitz, Ignition Partners
vendors.why_notable: Major independent iPaaS with 600+ 'Snaps'; data-heavy pipeline focus and one of the named alternatives in scope.

vendors.name: Jitterbit
vendors.one_liner: Harmony iPaaS for API, EDI, app and data integration with citizen-dev focus.
vendors.why_notable: Long-standing low-cost enterprise iPaaS; common MuleSoft/Boomi alternative for integration and EDI.

vendors.name: Nexla
vendors.one_liner: Automated data integration and 'Nexsets' platform for analytics and AI agents.
vendors.notable_investors: Blumberg Capital, Engineering Capital, Storm Ventures, Liberty Global Ventures, Shield Capital
vendors.why_notable: Automated integration used by DoorDash, LinkedIn, J&J; processes 1T+ records/month; explicitly named in scope.

vendors.name: Keboola
vendors.one_liner: End-to-end data operations platform unifying ingestion, transformation and orchestration.
vendors.why_notable: Popular European all-in-one ELT/data-stack platform; CDC and connectors; frequent Fivetran/Matillion alternative.

vendors.name: CloverDX
vendors.one_liner: Developer-friendly data integration platform for complex, custom ETL workflows.
vendors.why_notable: One of the original Java-based ETL frameworks; favored for granular control over complex transformation logic.

vendors.name: Integrate.io
vendors.one_liner: Fixed-fee no-code platform spanning ETL, ELT, CDC, reverse ETL and API generation.
vendors.funding_stage: Private (consolidation of Xplenty, FlyData, DreamFactory, Intermix)
vendors.why_notable: Full-spectrum pipeline platform with flat-fee pricing; consolidated several former ETL startups under one brand.

vendors.name: Skyvia
vendors.one_liner: Cloud no-code data integration, backup and access platform by Devart.
vendors.why_notable: 2,000+ paying customers across 120 countries; ~10B records/month; broad SMB ELT + backup coverage.

vendors.name: Dataddo
vendors.one_liner: No-code data integration platform with ETL, reverse ETL and replication.
vendors.hq: Mountain View, California, USA (founded Prague, Czech Republic)
vendors.why_notable: 300+ connectors, fully-managed, SOC 2 II / ISO 27001; strong in marketing/business-app data movement.

vendors.name: Hitachi Vantara (Pentaho)
vendors.one_liner: Pentaho Data Integration (Kettle): open-core ETL and analytics suite.
vendors.why_notable: Widely deployed open-core ETL (Kettle) with community + enterprise editions; on-prem heavyweight.

vendors.name: Confluent
vendors.one_liner: Kafka-based data streaming platform with connectors and stream processing.
vendors.why_notable: Founded by Kafka creators; 120+ connectors and streaming ETL; dominant real-time ingestion backbone.

vendors.name: StreamSets (an IBM company)
vendors.one_liner: DataOps platform for smart streaming/batch pipelines with data-drift handling.
vendors.why_notable: Pioneered data-drift-aware smart pipelines; still a distinct product brand for hybrid streaming/batch integration.

vendors.name: Upsolver
vendors.one_liner: No-code lakehouse ingestion engine for high-volume streaming into object storage.
vendors.notable_investors: Scale Venture Partners, Vertex Ventures, JVP, Wing Venture Capital
vendors.why_notable: Specializes in high-scale ingestion/upserts into S3/Iceberg lakehouses with SQL-based pipelines.

vendors.name: Streamkap
vendors.one_liner: Real-time streaming ETL/CDC platform (Kafka + Flink) with zero-ops sync.
vendors.notable_investors: Haatch, TEN13, Begin Capital, InReach Ventures
vendors.why_notable: Emerging sub-second CDC challenger replacing batch ETL; Postgres/MySQL/Mongo to Snowflake/Databricks.

vendors.name: Goldsky
vendors.one_liner: Real-time on-chain/blockchain data indexing and streaming pipelines.
vendors.notable_investors: Felicis, Dragonfly Capital, Elad Gil, Zach Perret, William Hockey
vendors.why_notable: Named in scope; subgraph + Mirror streaming pipelines productize CDC-style ingestion for blockchain data.

vendors.name: TapData
vendors.one_liner: Real-time database replication and operational data hub via CDC.
vendors.why_notable: APAC real-time CDC/replication player building live operational data hubs; Oracle/Mongo/Kafka sync.

vendors.name: Adverity
vendors.one_liner: Marketing/commerce data integration and analytics platform with governance.
vendors.notable_investors: SoftBank Vision Fund 2, Sapphire Ventures, Mangrove Capital
vendors.why_notable: Leading European marketing-data ETL platform (Unilever, IKEA, Bosch); strong connector + harmonization layer.

vendors.name: Funnel
vendors.one_liner: Marketing data hub that collects, normalizes and versions marketing data.
vendors.why_notable: Clean marketing data infrastructure with built-in storage and strong normalization; FT 1000 fast-grower.

vendors.name: Improvado
vendors.one_liner: Marketing data ETL plus managed services for agencies and enterprises.
vendors.why_notable: Combines ETL pipelines with a services layer; deep marketing/ad-platform connector coverage.

vendors.name: Saras Analytics (Daton)
vendors.one_liner: E-commerce-focused ELT (Daton) consolidating marketing/commerce data to warehouses.
vendors.why_notable: India-built, profitable ELT for Shopify/D2C brands; Daton is a low-cost Fivetran alternative for commerce.

vendors.name: Omnata
vendors.one_liner: Snowflake-native integration pushing/pulling data to operational SaaS apps.
vendors.why_notable: APAC warehouse-native connector vendor (Snowflake/Salesforce/Zendesk); bidirectional integration via Native App.

vendors.name: Weld
vendors.one_liner: Unified ELT, transformation and reverse-ETL platform with predictable pricing.
vendors.why_notable: European all-in-one modern-data-stack tool combining ingestion, dbt-style transforms and activation.

vendors.name: Y42
vendors.one_liner: Managed DataOps cloud for production data pipelines on BigQuery/Snowflake.
vendors.why_notable: End-to-end German platform unifying ingest/transform/orchestrate; aims to replace Fivetran+dbt+orchestration.

vendors.name: Peliqan
vendors.one_liner: All-in-one data platform: ELT connectors, built-in warehouse, SQL and activation.
vendors.why_notable: European low-code data platform bundling ingestion, warehouse and write-back for SMBs and SaaS embedding.

vendors.name: Lyftrondata
vendors.one_liner: Data pipeline and virtualization platform automating ELT into warehouses.
vendors.why_notable: 300+ connectors with data virtualization and pipeline automation; positioned as a Fivetran/Matillion alternative.

vendors.name: Datazoom
vendors.one_liner: Real-time video/streaming data collection and pipeline platform.
vendors.notable_investors: Build Group, Conductive Ventures, Hearst Ventures
vendors.why_notable: Specialized real-time data pipeline for video/observability telemetry routed to analytics destinations.

vendors.name: Datacoves
vendors.one_liner: Managed dbt + Airflow + ELT platform for enterprise analytics engineering.
vendors.why_notable: Enterprise managed modern-data-stack (dbt+Airflow+ingestion) emphasizing VPC deployment and governance.

vendors.name: Twilio Segment
vendors.one_liner: Pioneer CDP collecting, unifying and routing event data to 450+ destinations.
vendors.why_notable: The category-defining CDP; bought by Twilio for $3.2B, still the most widely deployed CDP.
vendors.notable_investors: Twilio (owner), prior: Accel, GV, Thrive, NEA

vendors.name: mParticle
vendors.one_liner: Real-time mobile-first CDP unifying and activating customer data across apps and web.
vendors.why_notable: Top enterprise CDP; acquired by Rokt for ~$300M (Jan 2025) but operates as a distinct product.
vendors.notable_investors: Rokt (owner); prior: Bain Capital Ventures, Social Capital

vendors.name: ActionIQ
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise warehouse-native CDP with visual audience builder across Snowflake/BigQuery/Databricks.
vendors.why_notable: Leading enterprise CDP; acquired by Uniphore (Dec 2024), rebranded CDP Agent, still sold to large brands.

vendors.name: Simon Data
vendors.one_liner: Warehouse-based composable CDP combining data activation with built-in messaging.
vendors.why_notable: Pioneered the 'fully-connected' CDP sitting on the warehouse; chosen by sophisticated lifecycle teams.

vendors.name: Blueshift
vendors.one_liner: AI-powered SmartHub CDP unifying data and orchestrating cross-channel campaigns.
vendors.why_notable: Combines CDP with predictive AI and omnichannel delivery; early mover on 'Customer AI' agents.
vendors.notable_investors: Fort Ross Ventures, Avatar Growth, Nexus Venture Partners, NEA, Storm Ventures

vendors.name: Lytics
vendors.one_liner: Real-time CDP for hyper-personalization, now powering Contentstack's composable DXP.
vendors.why_notable: CDP category pioneer (2013); acquired by Contentstack (closed Dec 2024) but remains a named CDP product.
vendors.notable_investors: Contentstack (owner); prior: JMI Equity, Two Sigma Ventures

vendors.name: Treasure Data
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise CDP unifying first-party data with zero-copy federated audience activation.
vendors.why_notable: One of the largest enterprise CDPs by deal size; ~$1B-valued, independent within SoftBank Vision Fund 2.

vendors.name: DinMo
vendors.one_liner: AI-powered composable CDP for European mid-market brands, warehouse-native activation.
vendors.why_notable: Rising EU composable-CDP challenger; privacy-first, serves Galeries Lafayette, Interflora, Ankorstore.
vendors.notable_investors: 468 Capital, Seedcamp, Motier Ventures, Financière Saint James

vendors.name: Multiwoven
vendors.one_liner: Open-source reverse ETL and composable CDP, self-hostable alternative to Hightouch/Census.
vendors.why_notable: Leading open-source reverse-ETL entrant (YC-backed); appeals to teams wanting self-hosting, no lock-in.

vendors.name: Bloomreach
vendors.one_liner: Commerce-focused CDP and marketing automation unifying online/offline customer data.
vendors.why_notable: $2.2B-valued; its Engagement CDP (ex-Exponea) is a top e-commerce CDP and Insider/Optimove rival.
vendors.notable_investors: Goldman Sachs Asset Management, Bain Capital Tech, Sixth Street

vendors.name: BlueConic
vendors.one_liner: Pure-play CDP unifying data into persistent profiles for cross-channel activation.
vendors.why_notable: Market-leading pure-play CDP (300+ brands: Heineken, ING, T-Mobile); Vista Equity-backed.
vendors.total_funding: Vista majority stake (2022); ~$60M+ prior venture funding
vendors.notable_investors: Vista Equity Partners, Spring Lake Equity, Sigma Prime Ventures

vendors.name: Optimove
vendors.one_liner: Real-time CDP with AI-led customer-led marketing and multichannel orchestration.
vendors.why_notable: Israeli CRM-marketing leader combining CDP + decisioning; strong in gaming, retail, finance.
vendors.hq: Tel Aviv, Israel / New York, NY

vendors.name: Insider
vendors.one_liner: AI-native CDP connecting siloed data and orchestrating experiences across 12+ channels.
vendors.why_notable: Unicorn; ranked #1 CDP on G2 Fall 2025; one of the few non-US (Turkey-origin) CDP unicorns.
vendors.notable_investors: Qatar Investment Authority, Sequoia Capital India, Riverwood, Wamda

vendors.name: Zeotap
vendors.one_liner: GDPR-first CDP with telco-verified identity graph and cookieless addressability.
vendors.why_notable: Leading European CDP treating GDPR as a structural default; strong identity/addressability play.

vendors.name: Optimizely Data Platform (Zaius)
vendors.one_liner: B2C commerce CDP (formerly Zaius) unifying behavioral data for personalization.
vendors.why_notable: The former Zaius CDP, now Optimizely's data layer; notable mid-market e-commerce CDP.

vendors.name: Acquia (Acquia CDP / AgilOne)
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise CDP (built on AgilOne) using ML for a single customer view.
vendors.why_notable: Acquia's CDP (ex-AgilOne) is a notable enterprise retail CDP; Vista-backed Drupal/DXP vendor.

vendors.name: SAP Engagement Cloud (Emarsys)
vendors.one_liner: Omnichannel marketing/engagement platform with CDP capabilities (formerly Emarsys).
vendors.why_notable: SAP's renamed Emarsys; major omnichannel engagement + customer-data layer for retail/commerce.

vendors.name: Pega Customer Decision Hub
vendors.one_liner: Real-time decisioning engine delivering next-best-action across channels.
vendors.why_notable: Leading enterprise real-time decisioning platform; the 'activation/decisioning' end of CDP stacks.

vendors.name: Adobe Real-Time CDP
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise real-time CDP on Adobe Experience Platform for personalization and activation.
vendors.why_notable: One of the two dominant enterprise CDPs (with Salesforce); deep martech ecosystem integration.

vendors.name: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Insights
vendors.one_liner: CDP within Dynamics 365 unifying profiles across CRM, Azure and Power Platform.
vendors.why_notable: Major enterprise CDP embedded in the Microsoft/Azure stack; broad installed base.

vendors.name: Lexer
vendors.one_liner: Retail-focused customer data platform unifying data to drive incremental sales.
vendors.why_notable: Leading ANZ retail CDP (~110 brands); one of the top four CDPs in the Australian market.

vendors.name: SALESmanago
vendors.one_liner: AI customer-engagement platform with CDP and marketing automation for e-commerce.
vendors.why_notable: Among Europe's fastest-growing marketing-automation/CDP vendors; large mid-market e-commerce base.

vendors.name: Voyado
vendors.one_liner: Retail/e-commerce customer experience platform combining CDP, loyalty and personalization.
vendors.why_notable: Leading Nordic retail CDP + loyalty platform; strong in European retail and fashion.

vendors.name: Ometria
vendors.one_liner: Customer data and experience platform for retailers, unifying data with cross-channel marketing.
vendors.why_notable: Well-funded UK retail CDP combining a CDP with experience/marketing orchestration.
vendors.notable_investors: Infravia Growth Capital, Octopus Ventures, Summit Action

vendors.name: Apteco
vendors.one_liner: Marketing data and analytics platform with CDP, segmentation and campaign capabilities.
vendors.why_notable: Long-established UK marketing-analytics/CDP vendor strong in data-driven CRM and segmentation.

vendors.name: MoEngage
vendors.one_liner: Insights-led customer engagement platform with built-in CDP for mobile-first brands.
vendors.why_notable: Well-funded global engagement platform (India-origin) with a native CDP; strong APAC/EMEA presence.
vendors.hq: San Francisco, CA, USA / Bengaluru, India
vendors.notable_investors: Goldman Sachs Asset Management, B Capital, Steadview, Eight Roads

vendors.name: CleverTap
vendors.one_liner: All-in-one engagement platform building unified real-time customer profiles.
vendors.why_notable: Unicorn engagement platform with CDP-style unified profiles; large mobile-first global base.
vendors.hq: Mountain View, CA, USA / Mumbai, India
vendors.notable_investors: CDPQ, Tiger Global, Sequoia Capital India, Accel

vendors.name: Klaviyo
vendors.one_liner: B2C marketing platform with a built-in CDP unifying e-commerce customer data.
vendors.why_notable: Public ($KVYO); its CDP unifies first-party data for SMB/mid-market e-commerce at huge scale.

vendors.name: Iterable
vendors.one_liner: Cross-lifecycle customer communication platform with data unification and activation.
vendors.why_notable: Well-funded growth-marketing platform increasingly positioned as an activation/CDP layer.
vendors.notable_investors: Silver Lake Waterman, Viking Global, CRV, Index Ventures

vendors.name: NextRoll (RollWorks/AdRoll)
vendors.one_liner: Data-driven marketing tech activating audiences across advertising for B2B and D2C.
vendors.why_notable: Long-standing martech with a large identity/audience graph used for cross-channel activation.
vendors.notable_investors: Foundation Capital, Institutional Venture Partners, Greylock

vendors.name: Castled Data
vendors.one_liner: Warehouse-native customer engagement and reverse-ETL syncing audiences from the warehouse.
vendors.why_notable: YC-backed warehouse-native player that pivoted from pure reverse ETL to marketer-facing activation.

vendors.name: Temporal Technologies
vendors.one_liner: Open-source durable execution engine for fault-tolerant, long-running workflows and agents.
vendors.why_notable: Category-defining durable-execution leader (open-source Temporal + Temporal Cloud); 183K+ weekly OSS devs, 2,500+ Cloud customers; the de-facto standard the rest of the field benchmarks against.
vendors.total_funding: ~$350M (raised $146M Series C 2025 at $1.72B; secondary in Oct 2025 lifted valuation to $2.5B)
vendors.notable_investors: Tiger Global, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Amplify Partners, GIC, MongoDB Ventures, 137 Ventures

vendors.name: Kestra
vendors.one_liner: Declarative, language-agnostic, event-driven orchestrator for data, AI and infra workflows.
vendors.why_notable: Fastest-growing European Airflow challenger; 30,000+ orgs, 26K+ GitHub stars, 2B+ workflows run in 2025; Kestra 2.0 adds distributed engine + agentic orchestration.
vendors.total_funding: ~$36M (€21M / $25M Series A in 2026, led by RTP Global)

vendors.name: Union.ai

vendors.one_liner: Fast operational BI and metrics dashboards for humans and agents.
vendors.notable_investors: True Ventures, Sierra Ventures, DCVC, Zero Prime Ventures
vendors.why_notable: BI-as-code on an embedded DuckDB engine; sub-second operational dashboards, ClickHouse/MotherDuck native.

vendors.name: Count
vendors.one_liner: AI-powered analytics canvas for metric trees and collaborative data exploration.
vendors.why_notable: Differentiated spatial-canvas BI for collaborative metric modeling and data storytelling.

vendors.name: Embeddable
vendors.one_liner: Developer toolkit for building custom, iframe-free embedded analytics.
vendors.why_notable: Emerging headless/code-first embedded analytics pure-play for ISVs; Nucleus 'Accelerator'.

vendors.name: Pinecone
vendors.one_liner: Fully managed serverless vector database for AI search and RAG.
vendors.notable_investors: Andreessen Horowitz, ICONIQ Growth, Menlo Ventures, Wing VC
vendors.why_notable: Category-defining managed vector DB; powers RAG at scale; reportedly weighing strategic options after Notion churn.

vendors.name: Weaviate
vendors.one_liner: Open-source AI-native vector database for search and generative apps.
vendors.notable_investors: Index Ventures, Battery Ventures, NEA, ING Ventures
vendors.why_notable: Leading open-source/EU vector DB with strong hybrid search and built-in generative modules.

vendors.name: Qdrant
vendors.one_liner: Open-source, Rust-built vector search engine and managed cloud.
vendors.notable_investors: Advance Venture Partners, Spark Capital, Unusual Ventures, 42CAP
vendors.why_notable: Fast-growing Rust-based engine positioning vector search as composable core infra for production AI.

vendors.name: Chroma
vendors.one_liner: Open-source embeddings database popular for building LLM/RAG apps.
vendors.notable_investors: Quiet Capital, AIX Ventures, Bloomberg Beta, Naval Ravikant, Nat Friedman
vendors.why_notable: Developer-favorite, easy-to-embed vector store; default choice in many LangChain/LLM prototypes.

vendors.name: Vespa.ai
vendors.one_liner: Open-source engine for vector, text and structured search at scale.
vendors.why_notable: Battle-tested Yahoo spinout serving big-data ranking/RAG; handles vectors plus full-text and ML ranking together.

vendors.name: LanceDB
vendors.one_liner: Open-source multimodal lakehouse and vector database for AI.
vendors.notable_investors: Theory Ventures, CRV, Databricks Ventures, Y Combinator
vendors.why_notable: Lance-format embedded/serverless vector DB pushing the 'multimodal lakehouse' for AI retrieval and training.

vendors.name: Marqo
vendors.one_liner: End-to-end vector search engine with built-in embedding generation.
vendors.why_notable: Bundles embedding inference with vector search (incl. multimodal) so teams skip the separate embedding pipeline.

vendors.name: Turbopuffer
vendors.one_liner: Serverless vector and full-text search built on object storage.
vendors.why_notable: Object-storage-native architecture gives very low cost at scale; powers search inside Cursor and Notion.

vendors.name: Activeloop
vendors.one_liner: Deep Lake database and AI data runtime for multimodal retrieval.
vendors.notable_investors: Streamlined Ventures, Y Combinator, Samsung Next, Dispersion Capital
vendors.why_notable: Deep Lake stores/streams multimodal data with vector search for deep-learning and agentic RAG workloads.

vendors.name: MyScale
vendors.one_liner: SQL vector database built on ClickHouse for AI applications.
vendors.why_notable: Combines vector, full-text and analytics in one ClickHouse-based SQL engine; open-sourced as MyScaleDB.

vendors.name: KX (KDB.AI)
vendors.one_liner: Time-aware multimodal vector database from the kdb+ maker.
vendors.hq: Newry, Northern Ireland / New York, USA
vendors.why_notable: Brings temporal + semantic relevance to vector search; leverages kdb+ pedigree in finance/real-time analytics.

vendors.name: TensorChord
vendors.one_liner: Postgres vector search extensions pgvecto.rs and VectorChord.
vendors.why_notable: VectorChord (successor to pgvecto.rs) delivers disk-efficient, high-throughput vector search inside Postgres.

vendors.name: Epsilla
vendors.one_liner: Fast open-source vector DB and private-knowledge AI-agent platform.
vendors.why_notable: Graph-traversal vector engine wrapped in an all-in-one RAG/agent builder for private data.

vendors.name: Vald
vendors.one_liner: Distributed, cloud-native approximate nearest-neighbor vector search engine.
vendors.notable_investors: n/a (backed by LY Corp / Yahoo! JAPAN)
vendors.why_notable: Kubernetes-native, horizontally-scalable ANN engine (NGT-based) proven at Yahoo! JAPAN billion-vector scale.

vendors.name: Superlinked
vendors.one_liner: Vector compute framework turning complex data into rich embeddings.
vendors.why_notable: The 'vector computer' layer that builds multi-attribute embeddings feeding any vector DB for better retrieval.

vendors.name: Mixedbread
vendors.one_liner: Open-source embedding and reranking models with search infra.
vendors.hq: San Francisco, CA, USA / Berlin, Germany
vendors.why_notable: Popular open-weight mxbai embedding/rerank models plus self-hosted retrieval inference for RAG stacks.

vendors.name: Vectara
vendors.one_liner: End-to-end enterprise RAG and agent platform with grounded answers.
vendors.why_notable: Full RAG pipeline (ingest→retrieve→rerank→generate) with built-in hallucination detection and its Mockingbird LLM.

vendors.name: Contextual AI
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise RAG 2.0 platform for grounded, accurate AI agents.
vendors.notable_investors: Greycroft, Bain Capital Ventures, NVIDIA (NVentures), Lightspeed
vendors.why_notable: Founded by RAG's originators; ships an integrated RAG 2.0 stack claiming large accuracy/efficiency gains for enterprises.

vendors.name: Vectorize
vendors.one_liner: Agentic RAG platform for production-ready retrieval pipelines.
vendors.why_notable: Automates chunking/embedding/indexing of unstructured data into optimized vector indexes for real-time RAG.

vendors.name: Ragie
vendors.one_liner: Fully managed RAG-as-a-service API for developers.
vendors.notable_investors: Craft Ventures, Chapter One, Saga VC
vendors.why_notable: Turnkey ingestion-to-retrieval API letting app teams add high-quality RAG without building the pipeline.

vendors.name: Needle
vendors.one_liner: AI search and agent platform with a managed RAG API.
vendors.why_notable: EU RAG API unlocking enterprise knowledge for agentic apps; connects data sources to retrieval out of the box.

vendors.name: Supabase
vendors.one_liner: Open-source Postgres backend with pgvector vector search built in.
vendors.notable_investors: Accel, GIC, Coatue, Y Combinator, Peak XV, Felicis
vendors.why_notable: Most popular pgvector front door; default vector store for the AI-coding/backend wave, now a $10.5B-valued platform.

vendors.name: MongoDB
vendors.one_liner: Document database with Atlas Vector Search and Voyage AI embeddings.
vendors.why_notable: Atlas Vector Search plus acquired Voyage AI embeddings make Mongo a major RAG-native competitor, not a partner.

vendors.name: Elastic
vendors.one_liner: Search platform with native dense/sparse vector search (ELSER).
vendors.hq: Mountain View, CA, USA / Amsterdam, NL
vendors.why_notable: Elasticsearch hybrid (BM25 + vector) plus acquired Jina AI embeddings; huge installed base for RAG retrieval.

vendors.name: Redis
vendors.one_liner: In-memory data platform with low-latency vector search.
vendors.notable_investors: Sequoia, Tiger Global, TCV, Goldman Sachs, Bain Capital
vendors.why_notable: Redis vector sets/search deliver sub-millisecond semantic retrieval; common as the hot RAG/semantic-cache layer.

vendors.name: Couchbase
vendors.one_liner: Multimodel database adding vector search for AI applications.
vendors.why_notable: Adds vector indexing to its JSON/full-text engine for hybrid operational RAG; being taken private by Haveli.

vendors.name: Oracle
vendors.one_liner: Oracle Database 23ai with built-in AI Vector Search.
vendors.why_notable: Native vectors in Oracle Database 23ai let enterprises run RAG on existing relational data; a heavyweight competitor.

vendors.name: Anthropic
vendors.one_liner: Frontier LLM lab building the Claude family of models and APIs.
vendors.notable_investors: Sequoia, Altimeter, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, Google, Amazon, Lightspeed
vendors.why_notable: ~$965B valuation; Claude Code drove a ~$47B revenue run-rate; closest frontier rival to OpenAI/Google.

vendors.name: Google DeepMind (Gemini)
vendors.one_liner: Google's AI division building the Gemini frontier multimodal model family.
vendors.hq: London, UK / Mountain View, CA, USA
vendors.why_notable: Gemini is one of three true frontier model families; TPU stack + Vertex AI give Google end-to-end vertical integration.

vendors.name: Cohere
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise and sovereign LLM platform with Command models and embeddings.
vendors.notable_investors: Radical Ventures, Inovia, NVIDIA, AMD, Salesforce, PSP, Schwarz Group
vendors.why_notable: ~$240M ARR; deployment-agnostic enterprise focus; acquiring Germany's Aleph Alpha (~$20B combined) to anchor a transatlantic sovereign-AI play.

vendors.name: Mistral AI
vendors.one_liner: European frontier lab shipping open-weight and commercial LLMs.
vendors.notable_investors: ASML (lead), a16z, DST Global, General Catalyst, Index, Lightspeed, NVIDIA, Bpifrance
vendors.why_notable: Europe's flagship frontier lab; le Chat assistant + open weights; ASML-led round signals strategic EU sovereignty bet.

vendors.name: xAI
vendors.one_liner: Elon Musk's lab building the Grok models, integrated into X.
vendors.notable_investors: Valor Equity, Fidelity, Qatar Investment Authority, NVIDIA, Cisco
vendors.why_notable: Valued ~$250B before merging with SpaceX; Grok reaches ~600M X/Grok MAUs; Colossus supercluster is among the largest training builds.

vendors.name: Meta (Llama)
vendors.one_liner: Meta's Superintelligence Labs producing the open-weight Llama model family.
vendors.why_notable: Llama is the most-downloaded open-weight family and the de-facto base for countless derivatives; sets the open-model bar globally.

vendors.name: Together AI
vendors.one_liner: AI acceleration cloud for training and serving open-source models.
vendors.notable_investors: General Catalyst, Prosperity7, NVIDIA, Kleiner Perkins, Lux, Coatue, Salesforce Ventures
vendors.why_notable: Hit ~$1B annualized revenue (Feb 2026); leading neutral GPU+inference cloud for open models; deploying tens of thousands of Blackwell GPUs.

vendors.name: Fireworks AI
vendors.one_liner: Fast, low-cost inference and fine-tuning platform for open models.
vendors.total_funding: $250M Series C at $4B (Oct 2025); in talks at ~$15B
vendors.notable_investors: Lightspeed, Index Ventures, Sequoia, NVIDIA, AMD, Databricks Ventures, Benchmark
vendors.why_notable: ~$800M annualized revenue, 10,000+ customers; speed-optimized open-model inference; valuation nearly 4x in seven months.

vendors.name: DeepSeek
vendors.one_liner: Chinese lab shipping highly efficient open-weight frontier models.
vendors.total_funding: Self-funded by High-Flyer; ~$45-50B round reportedly in talks
vendors.notable_investors: High-Flyer (parent); China state AI fund / Tencent reportedly in talks
vendors.why_notable: V3/R1 reset global cost expectations; MIT-licensed V4 (1.6T params, 1M context); the most influential Chinese open-weight lab.

vendors.name: Aleph Alpha
vendors.one_liner: German sovereign-AI lab building Luminous/Pharia models for regulated sectors.
vendors.notable_investors: Schwarz Group, Bosch, SAP, Hewlett Packard Enterprise; Cohere (acquirer)
vendors.why_notable: Europe's leading sovereign-AI lab for defense/finance/public sector; being absorbed by Cohere but still operating as a distinct German brand.

vendors.name: Reka AI
vendors.one_liner: Frontier-class multimodal LLMs across text, image, video and audio.
vendors.notable_investors: NVIDIA, Snowflake, DST Global, Radical Ventures
vendors.why_notable: DeepMind/FAIR alumni team; Core/Flash/Edge models plus Reka Vision; on-device multimodal and physical-security deployments.

vendors.name: Stability AI
vendors.one_liner: Generative-media foundation models, best known for Stable Diffusion.
vendors.notable_investors: Eric Schmidt, Coatue, Lightspeed, Greycroft, Sean Parker, WPP
vendors.why_notable: Pioneered open image generation; recapitalized under CEO Prem Akkaraju; expanding into 3D, audio and video foundation models.

vendors.name: Hugging Face
vendors.one_liner: Open model/dataset hub and Transformers library; the GitHub of ML.
vendors.notable_investors: Salesforce, Google, Amazon, NVIDIA, Intel, AMD, Qualcomm, IBM, Sequoia, Lux
vendors.why_notable: Hosts 1M+ models and is the default distribution layer for open models; ships its own SmolLM/SmolVLM and serverless inference.

vendors.name: Perplexity AI
vendors.one_liner: AI answer engine plus in-house Sonar models and an agentic browser.
vendors.notable_investors: NVIDIA, SoftBank Vision Fund 2, IVP, Accel, NEA, Bessemer, Jeff Bezos
vendors.why_notable: ~45M MAUs and ~$200M ARR; trains its own Sonar models and ships the Comet browser, blurring model lab and consumer search.

vendors.name: AI21 Labs
vendors.one_liner: Israeli enterprise LLM lab behind the Jamba (Mamba-Transformer) models.
vendors.notable_investors: NVIDIA, Google, Samsung, Intel Capital, Pitango, Walden Catalyst
vendors.why_notable: Jamba hybrid SSM-Transformer architecture with long context; Maestro agent stack; reportedly in NVIDIA acquisition talks ($2-3B).

vendors.name: Safe Superintelligence (SSI)
vendors.one_liner: Ilya Sutskever's lab pursuing safe superintelligence as its sole product.
vendors.hq: Palo Alto, CA, USA / Tel Aviv, Israel
vendors.notable_investors: a16z, Sequoia, DST Global, Greenoaks, Alphabet, NVIDIA
vendors.why_notable: Highest-valued lab with no shipped product; founded by OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever; pure frontier-AGI bet.

vendors.name: Thinking Machines Lab
vendors.one_liner: Mira Murati's lab building real-time, multimodal interaction models.
vendors.notable_investors: Andreessen Horowitz, NVIDIA, AMD, Cisco, Jane Street
vendors.why_notable: Founded by ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati with a top researcher roster; shipping Tinker + low-latency interaction models; gigawatt-scale compute deals.

vendors.name: Reflection AI
vendors.one_liner: DeepMind alumni building America's open-weight frontier models.
vendors.notable_investors: NVIDIA (lead), Lightspeed, Sequoia, DST Global, GIC
vendors.why_notable: Explicitly positioned as the Western open-weight answer to DeepSeek; founding member of NVIDIA's Nemotron Coalition; led by AlphaGo/Gemini veterans.

vendors.name: Liquid AI
vendors.one_liner: MIT spinout building efficient non-transformer Liquid Foundation Models.
vendors.notable_investors: AMD, Duke Capital Partners, OSS Capital, PagsGroup
vendors.why_notable: Liquid Neural Network architecture delivers frontier-grade quality on-device at a fraction of compute; LFM2 targets edge/embedded agents.

vendors.name: Writer
vendors.one_liner: Full-stack enterprise generative-AI platform with in-house Palmyra LLMs.
vendors.notable_investors: Premji Invest, Radical Ventures, ICONIQ Growth, Salesforce Ventures, IBM Ventures, Workday Ventures
vendors.why_notable: Trains its own Palmyra models (incl. domain-specific Med/Fin) rather than reselling; agentic enterprise focus with strong net retention.

vendors.name: Inflection AI
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise/government LLM lab after its founders moved to Microsoft.
vendors.notable_investors: Microsoft, NVIDIA, Reid Hoffman, Bill Gates, Eric Schmidt
vendors.why_notable: Reorganized under CEO Sean White after the Microsoft talent deal; pivoted from consumer Pi to on-prem enterprise/government model deployments.

vendors.name: Zhipu AI (Z.ai)
vendors.one_liner: Chinese frontier lab behind the open-weight GLM model family.
vendors.notable_investors: Prosperity7, Qiming, Hillhouse, Tencent, Alibaba, Saudi Aramco fund
vendors.why_notable: First major Chinese LLM lab to IPO; GLM-5 is a top-ranked open-weight frontier model; Tsinghua-rooted 'AI Tiger.'

vendors.name: Moonshot AI
vendors.one_liner: Beijing lab behind the open-weight Kimi (K2) long-context models.
vendors.notable_investors: Alibaba, Tencent, HongShan, IDG Capital, Meituan/Long-Z, ZhenFund
vendors.why_notable: China's top-funded LLM startup; Kimi K2 is among the most-used models on OpenRouter; surging overseas API revenue (~$200M ARR).

vendors.name: MiniMax
vendors.one_liner: Chinese multimodal lab spanning text, audio, video and consumer apps.
vendors.notable_investors: Alibaba, Tencent, HongShan, Hillhouse, IDG, Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, miHoYo
vendors.why_notable: Strong multimodal/voice/video models plus hit consumer apps (Talkie); 200M+ cumulative users; second Chinese LLM lab to IPO.

vendors.name: StepFun
vendors.one_liner: Shanghai multimodal lab shipping text, image, video and voice models.
vendors.notable_investors: Tencent, Qiming Venture Partners, 5Y Capital, Shanghai SDIC, China Life
vendors.why_notable: Largest Asian AI raise of the quarter; 16+ multimodal models; one of China's 'AI Tigers' with a planned Hong Kong IPO.

vendors.name: Baichuan Intelligence
vendors.one_liner: Beijing LLM lab now focused on medical/healthcare foundation models.
vendors.notable_investors: Alibaba, Tencent, Xiaomi, Shenzhen & Beijing government funds
vendors.why_notable: Sogou founder Wang Xiaochuan's lab; pivoted to medical AI with Baichuan-M3 claiming sub-3% hallucination; IPO targeted ~2027.

vendors.name: 01.AI
vendors.one_liner: Kai-Fu Lee's lab behind the bilingual open-weight Yi model family.
vendors.notable_investors: Alibaba, Sinovation Ventures, Xiaomi, Legend Capital
vendors.why_notable: Yi models are strong bilingual EN-CN open weights; founder Kai-Fu Lee; shifted toward enterprise solutions while keeping open releases.

vendors.name: Alibaba (Qwen)
vendors.one_liner: Alibaba's Qwen team ships one of the most-used open-weight model families.
vendors.why_notable: Qwen is the most-derived-from open-weight family globally with thousands of fine-tunes; spans dense, MoE and multimodal variants on ModelScope/Hugging Face.

vendors.name: Technology Innovation Institute (Falcon)
vendors.one_liner: Abu Dhabi research institute producing the open-weight Falcon models.
vendors.notable_investors: Advanced Technology Research Council (Abu Dhabi government)
vendors.why_notable: Falcon 40B/180B and Falcon-H1 are leading Apache-2.0 open models; flagship of UAE/G42 sovereign-AI ambitions with strong Arabic capability.

vendors.name: Allen Institute for AI (Ai2)
vendors.one_liner: Nonprofit lab releasing fully-open OLMo, Molmo and Tulu models.
vendors.total_funding: Paul Allen endowment; $152M NSF/NVIDIA compute project
vendors.notable_investors: Paul Allen estate, NSF, NVIDIA (compute partners)
vendors.why_notable: Gold standard for fully-open models (weights + data + training code); OLMo 3 includes the first fully-open 32B reasoning model; Molmo for multimodal.

vendors.name: Sarvam AI
vendors.one_liner: India's sovereign-AI lab building Indic-language foundation models.
vendors.notable_investors: Lightspeed, Peak XV, Khosla Ventures, NVIDIA, Accel, HCLTech (reported)
vendors.why_notable: Chosen under the IndiaAI Mission to build India's sovereign LLM; released Apache-2.0 Sarvam-30B/105B; voice-first Indic-language focus.

vendors.name: Black Forest Labs
vendors.one_liner: German lab behind the FLUX image-generation foundation models.
vendors.notable_investors: Salesforce Ventures, a16z, NVIDIA, General Catalyst, Temasek, AMP
vendors.why_notable: FLUX is the leading open/commercial image model, embedded by Adobe, Canva, Meta and Microsoft; one of Europe's most valuable AI companies; Nemotron Coalition member.

vendors.name: Krutrim
vendors.one_liner: Ola's Indian AI venture with Krutrim LLMs, now pivoting to AI cloud.
vendors.why_notable: India's first GenAI unicorn; open-sourced Indic models; has shifted emphasis to AI cloud/infrastructure while pausing in-house chip and frontier-model work.

vendors.name: DataRobot
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise AI platform for AutoML, model deployment, monitoring and generative AI governance.
vendors.why_notable: One of the original AutoML/MLOps category leaders; ~$1B+ raised, peak $6.3B valuation. Pivoted hard to enterprise agentic + predictive AI governance.
vendors.notable_investors: NEA, Sapphire Ventures, Tiger Global, Altimeter, Meritech

vendors.name: H2O.ai
vendors.one_liner: Open-source-rooted AI platform spanning AutoML (Driverless AI), LLMs and agentic AI.
vendors.why_notable: 20,000+ organizations; strong open-source heritage (H2O-3) plus enterprise air-gapped deployment for government/finance. A core Dataiku/DataRobot-class competitor.
vendors.notable_investors: Goldman Sachs, NVIDIA, Wells Fargo, Commonwealth Bank, Nexus Venture Partners

vendors.name: Domino Data Lab
vendors.one_liner: Enterprise MLOps platform for collaborative, reproducible model development across hybrid/multi-cloud.
vendors.why_notable: Go-to enterprise data-science platform for regulated industries; ~$224M raised, Series F in 2025. Strong governance, reproducibility and on-prem/hybrid story.
vendors.notable_investors: Sequoia, Coatue, Highland Capital, NVIDIA, Snowflake Ventures

vendors.name: Weights & Biases
vendors.one_liner: Developer-first platform for experiment tracking, model lineage and LLM/agent evaluation.
vendors.why_notable: Industry-standard experiment tracking, trusted by OpenAI, Cohere, Midjourney. Acquired by CoreWeave (~$1.7B, 2025) but still operates as the leading AI developer platform brand.
vendors.notable_investors: Coatue, Insight Partners, Felicis, a16z (now CoreWeave)

vendors.name: Comet
vendors.one_liner: ML experiment tracking, model registry and LLM evaluation (Opik) system of record.
vendors.why_notable: Leading independent experiment-tracking/observability vendor handling both classic ML and LLM workflows; Opik open-source LLM-eval tool gained strong traction.
vendors.notable_investors: OpenView, Scale Venture Partners, Trilogy Equity Partners, Two Sigma Ventures

vendors.name: ClearML
vendors.one_liner: Open-source-based end-to-end MLOps and AI infrastructure (orchestration, experiments, GPU scheduling).
vendors.why_notable: 1,300+ enterprise customers; strong open-source adoption plus fractional-GPU/Kubernetes scheduling positioning it as an AI infra control plane. Roots in Allegro AI (Israel).
vendors.hq: San Diego, CA, USA / Tel Aviv, Israel
vendors.notable_investors: MizMaa Ventures, Samsung Catalyst, Hyundai CRADLE, Bosch Ventures

vendors.name: Hopsworks
vendors.one_liner: End-to-end feature store and ML platform with strong lineage, governance and real-time serving.
vendors.why_notable: Default feature-store choice for regulated industries (finance, healthcare); tight integration of feature store + model management. European (Swedish) origin.
vendors.hq: Palo Alto, CA, USA / Stockholm, Sweden

vendors.name: Featureform
vendors.one_liner: Virtual/open-source feature store that orchestrates existing data infra into a feature platform.
vendors.why_notable: Engineering-first 'virtual' feature store that decouples compute/storage; popular open-source project for teams avoiding a hosted store.

vendors.name: Feast
vendors.one_liner: Leading open-source feature store for serving ML features online and offline.
vendors.why_notable: The de facto open-source feature store standard (originated at Gojek); community-governed with broad ecosystem adoption and an active LF AI & Data project.

vendors.name: Tecton
vendors.one_liner: Managed real-time feature platform for production ML, built by the creators of Uber Michelangelo.
vendors.why_notable: Gold-standard managed feature/real-time data platform for ML; acquired by Databricks (Aug 2025) but still operates as the leading streaming feature store.
vendors.notable_investors: Kleiner Perkins, a16z, Sequoia, Bain Capital Ventures, Tiger Global

vendors.name: Seldon
vendors.one_liner: Kubernetes-native MLOps platform for deploying, serving and monitoring ML models at scale.
vendors.why_notable: Leading open-source-rooted (Seldon Core) model-serving/deployment stack for platform/DevOps teams; strong in advanced inference graphs and the enterprise MLServer/Core 2 line.
vendors.notable_investors: Bright Pixel (Sonae IM), AlbionVC, Cambridge Innovation Capital

vendors.name: BentoML
vendors.one_liner: Open-source framework and cloud for packaging and serving ML/AI models as scalable APIs.
vendors.why_notable: Hugely popular open-source model-serving framework; BentoCloud and the open-source project are a default 'model-to-API' path, increasingly used for LLM/compound-AI serving.

vendors.name: Valohai
vendors.one_liner: End-to-end managed MLOps platform with pipeline orchestration across any cloud or on-prem.
vendors.why_notable: Established European (Finnish) MLOps platform focused on reproducible pipelines and 'knowledge repository' lineage; popular with regulated and on-prem ML teams.
vendors.notable_investors: Superhero Capital, Reaktor Ventures, Business Finland

vendors.name: Anyscale
vendors.one_liner: Managed platform for Ray — distributed compute for training, batch and scalable model serving.
vendors.why_notable: Commercial steward of Ray, the distributed-compute backbone behind much modern AI infra (incl. OpenAI scaling history); $1B+ valuation. End-to-end scale-and-serve story.
vendors.notable_investors: a16z, NEA, Addition, Intel Capital, Foundation Capital

vendors.name: Baseten
vendors.one_liner: Inference platform for deploying and scaling ML/LLM models with fast cold starts and autoscaling.
vendors.why_notable: Fast-rising dedicated inference provider; $300M Series E at ~$5B valuation (2026). Truss open-source packaging + Model APIs make it a leading independent serving player.
vendors.notable_investors: IVP, Spark Capital, Greylock, Premji Invest, BOND

vendors.name: Modal
vendors.one_liner: Serverless cloud for running Python/AI workloads and GPU inference without managing infrastructure.
vendors.why_notable: Developer-loved serverless GPU platform; $87M Series B at $1.1B valuation (2025). Increasingly the default for custom-model training and inference at scale.
vendors.notable_investors: Lux Capital, Redpoint, Definition Capital, Amplify Partners

vendors.name: Replicate
vendors.one_liner: API platform to run and share open-source ML models packaged with the Cog container tool.
vendors.why_notable: Lowest-friction 'model-to-API' marketplace; widely used for running and fine-tuning open-source models. Cog is a popular open-source packaging standard.

vendors.name: TrueFoundry

vendors.name: UbiOps

vendors.name: Deepchecks

vendors.name: Aporia

vendors.name: Superwise

vendors.name: Arize AI

vendors.name: Pecan AI

vendors.name: Abacus.AI

vendors.name: Inferless

vendors.why_notable: Emerging serverless-inference startup (Delaware/Bengaluru) claiming minute-scale deploys and large cost savings; backed by top-tier VCs. Notable India-linked serving player.

vendors.name: Aible

vendors.name: cnvrg.io

vendors.name: Pachyderm

vendors.name: DagsHub

vendors.name: OpenMetal / KServe (community)

vendors.name: Predibase

vendors.name: LangChain

vendors.name: AG2 (AG2AI)

vendors.name: deepset (Haystack)

vendors.name: CoRover
vendors.one_liner: Indian conversational and generative-AI platform (BharatGPT) for multilingual virtual agents.
vendors.why_notable: BharatGPT-powered multilingual agent platform with large Indian public-sector deployments (IRCTC etc.).

vendors.name: GreptimeDB
vendors.one_liner: Open-source cloud-native time-series/observability database unifying metrics, logs and events.
vendors.why_notable: Rust-based unified time-series database with strong APAC roots; a distinct OLAP/observability-store entrant.

vendors.name: Lantern
vendors.one_liner: Postgres-native vector database extension and cloud for building RAG and AI search on existing Postgres.
vendors.why_notable: High-performance pgvector alternative (HNSW + external indexing) as a Postgres-native RAG layer.

vendors.name: Databrain
vendors.one_liner: Embedded analytics platform for quickly adding customer-facing dashboards and AI insights to SaaS products.
vendors.why_notable: Lightweight embedded-analytics SDK with India-based roots; a Luzmo/Embeddable peer for SaaS teams.

vendors.name: E2B
vendors.one_liner: Open-source secure cloud sandboxes for running AI-generated code and agent actions at scale.
vendors.why_notable: Widely used sandbox/runtime for agentic code execution; core infra layer for the agent ecosystem.

vendors.name: Browserbase
vendors.one_liner: Managed headless-browser infrastructure for AI agents to browse and act on the web (Stagehand).
vendors.why_notable: Leading browser-automation infra for web agents (creators of Stagehand); a fast-growing agent-tooling layer.

vendors.name: Didomi
vendors.one_liner: European consent-management and privacy platform for GDPR/CCPA preference and data governance.
vendors.why_notable: Major European CMP/privacy vendor; strengthens EU consent-and-governance coverage (OneTrust/Osano peer).

vendors.name: Northern Data Group
vendors.one_liner: European HPC/GPU cloud provider (Taiga Cloud) offering large-scale NVIDIA capacity for AI training and inference.
vendors.category: AI Infrastructure: Inference, GPU & Model Serving
vendors.why_notable: One of Europe's largest GPU-cloud operators; key sovereign EU AI-infra capacity provider.
vendors.segment: AI Infrastructure: Inference, GPU & Model Serving

vendors.name: Krutrim Cloud
vendors.one_liner: Indian AI cloud offering GPU compute and inference for sovereign models, from Ola's Krutrim.
vendors.category: AI Infrastructure: Inference, GPU & Model Serving
vendors.why_notable: India's first AI-unicorn cloud arm building domestic GPU/inference infrastructure; distinct from Krutrim's models.
vendors.segment: AI Infrastructure: Inference, GPU & Model Serving

vendors.name: Gramener
vendors.one_liner: India-based data science, analytics and data-storytelling consultancy (now part of Straive).
vendors.why_notable: Well-known Indian analytics/visualization consultancy; complements the India-heavy SI roster with a viz focus.

vendors.name: Recordly
vendors.one_liner: Nordic data-engineering and analytics consultancy specializing in modern data platforms (Snowflake/Databricks/dbt).
vendors.why_notable: Notable Finnish/Nordic data-platform consultancy; broadens non-US SI coverage in Northern Europe.

vendors.name: Pika
vendors.one_liner: Generative-AI video model and consumer creation platform for short AI video.
vendors.why_notable: Prominent text/image-to-video foundation-model startup; a Runway/Luma peer in generative video.

vendors.name: Tencent Hunyuan
vendors.one_liner: Tencent's foundation-model family spanning text, image, video and 3D generation.
vendors.why_notable: Major Chinese multimodal foundation models with open-weight releases; a key APAC model provider omitted from the list.

vendors.name: iFlytek
vendors.one_liner: Chinese AI company behind the Spark (Xinghuo) LLM and leading speech/voice AI technology.
vendors.why_notable: Major Chinese LLM (Spark) and dominant speech-AI provider; a significant APAC omission.

vendors.name: Chunkr
vendors.one_liner: Open-source document-ingestion API that converts PDFs/docs into structured, chunked data for RAG.
vendors.why_notable: Developer-focused layout-aware document parsing/chunking for RAG; an Unstructured/Reducto peer.

vendors.name: Extend
vendors.one_liner: AI document-processing platform turning complex documents into structured data with human-in-the-loop review.
vendors.why_notable: Modern LLM-native intelligent document processing; a Hyperscience/Reducto-class entrant for data extraction.
